#volume_levels: 0 counts as a volume level, so when volume_levels=50 the max volume level is 49
#dimensions: natural width and height of the vumeter
#files: The set of images required to render this VU-Meter
#resources: associative array of image resources, which serve as symbolic links
to image file names and their dimensions
#placement: placement is an associative array of image aliases to coordinates
each placement specifies where an image associated with an alias will be rendered
#backgrounds: associative arrays keyed on background name
contents of each associative array are associative arrays of channels
channels with names other the 'left' and 'right' or common, i.e. rendered always
each channels is an array of placements - there is no volume level associated with these placements
#levels*: Associative arrays keyed on level name, of level associative arrays with keys 'left' and 'right'
The contents of each key must be objects which are associative arrays with 3 keys
'single' => only render the placement matching the volume level
'aggregate' => render all placements upto the volume level
'aggregateoff' => render all placements beyond the volume level
'hold'  : peak volume level is held for a brief time and rendered in non-aggregated manner,
lower volume levels are rendered to match sampled values.
For use with digital VU Meters, where multiple volume level can be rendered simultaneously
'decay' : (default) volume levels follows peak volume level decaying at configured rate, if lower
For use with 'analog' VU Meters simulating needle movement
'hold+decay': peak volume level is held for a brief time and rendered in non-aggregated manner,
lower volume levels follows peak volume level decaying at configured rate
if peak is not set then volume levels are rendered to match sampled values
'placements': an associative arrays mapping volume level to placements
Each object does not have to contain placements for all volume levels
However the combination of objects SHOULD contain placements for all volume levels
if not then absent volume levels will not be rendered
vumeters#: Associate array of VU Meters defined in this file keyed on VU Meter name
In turn contents are associative arrays containing 2 keys
levels: which set of levels in layout to use for the VU Meter. REQUIRED
background: which background in layout to use, if absent then no background is rendered
